profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner and primary point of contact; tends to drive retirement timing discussions and keeps the household on track.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing account types (taxable vs. retirement) and taxes at a high level, but prefers the advisor to translate strategy into clear next steps.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Growth-oriented but pragmatic: willing to stay invested for long-term goals, yet becomes cautious when conversations turn to near-term retirement income reliability and market drawdowns.
profile.communication_style: Direct and organized, but uses hedging when recalling details ("I think," "roughly," "it’s around..."). Speaks in mostly exact figures when she has statements, otherwise gives ranges; asks clarifying questions and wants a recap at the end.
emotional_tendencies: mild anxiety about timing retirement correctly
relief when there is a clear plan and checklist
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical with a deadline: gathers information, compares options, then commits once she understands tradeoffs and the plan feels implementable.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to collaborate, but expects competence and transparency on fees, taxes, and why recommendations fit their situation.
internal_biases: loss aversion near retirement (more sensitive to downturns now than earlier in career)
anchoring to current spending level as the baseline for retirement planning
hidden_concerns: worries that "fair" health could force earlier retirement or higher medical costs
concerned about paying unnecessary taxes in the 32% bracket and wants tax-smart income planning

profile.name: Michael
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; supportive participant who focuses on day-to-day operations and lifestyle preferences, often deferring on investment specifics.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands budgeting and the idea of diversification, but mixes up terminology (gross vs. net, balance vs. contribution) and prefers plain-English explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative in the moment: tends to ask "what if the market drops" questions and favors keeping a noticeable cash buffer, even if he agrees with a growth allocation in principle.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-driven; uses round numbers ("about six hundred," "a little over half a million") and occasional filler ("you know," "kind of"). Will interrupt briefly to clarify a term, then lets Elizabeth finish.
emotional_tendencies: frustration if the conversation gets too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-based: wants to hear the advisor’s recommendation, then checks in with Elizabeth; prefers a small number of clear options rather than many scenarios.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive: trusts professionals but wants to feel heard; confidence increases when the advisor summarizes and confirms understanding.
internal_biases: availability bias (recent market headlines influence his comfort level)
status quo bias (prefers minimal changes unless the benefit is obvious)
hidden_concerns: concerned about maintaining lifestyle with no debt but rising costs in retirement
quietly worried about healthcare and longevity planning given both report fair health
